sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value DOUBLE, ts TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE);
INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (1, 5.0, transaction_timestamp()), (2, NULL, transaction_timestamp()), (3, 0.0, transaction_timestamp());
SELECT id, value, transaction_timestamp() AS current_time, (value ! 3) AS factorial_value FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_data WHERE value < 10) AS max_value, transaction_timestamp() AS current_time FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING (max_value ! 2) > 0;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_data AS SELECT id, value, ts FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY ts DESC;
SELECT temp.id, temp.value, (temp.value ! 1) * 2 AS complex_factorial FROM temp_data temp JOIN test_data main ON temp.id = main.id WHERE main.ts > transaction_timestamp() - INTERVAL '1 hour';
SELECT ts, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NULL) AS null_count, (transaction_timestamp() ! 1) AS current_factorial FROM test_data GROUP BY ts HAVING COUNT(value) < 2;
UPDATE test_data SET value = value ! 0 WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, (transaction_timestamp() ! 3) AS current_time_plus_factorial FROM test_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE ts < transaction_timestamp());

SELECT temp.id, (temp.value ! 1) AS factorial_one, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS non_null_count FROM temp_data temp LEFT JOIN test_data ON temp.id = test_data.id WHERE test_data.value IS NULL;
